# orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_itsm_template (Resource)

Manages a ServiceNow **ITSM template** in Orca Security.

Orca splits the ServiceNow integration into two pieces:

1. The **resource** (managed by
   [`orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_resource`](integration_servicenow_resource.md)) — holds
   the ServiceNow credentials. The same credentials back both ITSM and SIR
   templates.
2. The **template** (this resource) — holds the per-ticket settings: which instance
   to open incidents in, the alert→incident field mapping, the resolution code/note,
   the reopen state, etc. One credential can back many templates.

Under the hood this creates an entry under `POST /api/external_service/config`
with `service_name = "sn_incidents"` and `config.type = "ITSM"`. Updates go to
`PUT /api/external_service/config/sn_incidents?template=<template_name>`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Credentials side of the integration — created separately.
resource "orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_resource" "creds" {
  name           = "servicenow-itsm-prod"
  servicenow_url = "https://ven03666.service-now.com"
  username       = "username"
  password       = var.servicenow_password
}

# Template that defines how Orca alerts map to ServiceNow incident fields and how
# resolution/reopen events behave.
resource "orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_itsm_template" "demo" {
  template_name = "teamplate_name"
  resource_id   = orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_resource.creds.id
  instance_name = "instance_name"
  username      = "username"

  resolution_status = "6"
  resolution_code   = "Resolved by caller"
  resolution_note   = "Resolved by @someone"
  reopen_status     = "7"

  # List values: a bare string pulls an Orca alert field; `{ value = "<literal>" }` is a
  # static value.
  mapping_json = jsonencode({
    category        = [{ value = "software" }]
    u_risk_name     = ["alert_name"]
    u_subscription  = []
    business_impact = ["details"]
  })

  is_enabled = true
  is_default = false
}
```

## Argument Reference

* `template_name` — (Required, String) Identifier for the template, used as the URL key
  for updates and deletes. Changing this forces a new resource.
* `resource_id` — (Optional, String) ID of the
  `orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_resource` resource that carries the credentials.
* `instance_name` — (Optional, String) ServiceNow instance subdomain (for example,
  `ven03666`). Mutually exclusive with `base_url`. Required when no `resource_id` is
  given.
* `base_url` — (Optional, String) Full ServiceNow base URL (`https://...`). Mutually
  exclusive with `instance_name`. Takes precedence over `instance_name` if both are
  set.
* `username` — (Optional, String) ServiceNow username override. Usually inherited
  from the linked resource.
* `resolution_status` — (Optional, String) ServiceNow incident state code to use
  when Orca resolves a ticket.
* `resolution_code` — (Optional, String) ServiceNow close code.
* `resolution_note` — (Optional, String) ServiceNow close notes.
* `reopen_status` — (Optional, String) Incident state code Orca moves a ticket to
  when re-opening it.
* `mapping_json` — (Optional, String) JSON-encoded `mapping` object. Each key is a
  ServiceNow field name and the value is a list where a **bare string** pulls an Orca
  alert field (shorthand for `{ "orca": "<field>" }`) and `{ "value": "<literal>" }`
  is a static value. Use `jsonencode({...})` to keep the HCL readable.
* `on_close_alert_mapping_json` — (Optional, String) JSON-encoded
  `on_close_alert_mapping` object used when an Orca-driven close event syncs back
  to ServiceNow.
* `allow_reopen_and_resolution` — (Optional, Bool) Default `false`.
* `allow_mapping` — (Optional, Bool) Default `false`.
* `is_enabled` — (Optional, Bool) Default `true`.
* `is_default` — (Optional, Bool) Default `false`.

> **Note:** Orca does not accept `business_units` on this endpoint — the UI
> does not send it either. To scope a template to specific business units,
> manage the scoping at the credentials resource (`orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_resource`)
> level or via Orca's RBAC.

## Attribute Reference

* `id` — (String) Orca external service config identifier (UUID).

## Import

```bash
terraform import orcasecurity_integration_servicenow_itsm_template.example template_name
```
